name: Cavalier King Charles Spaniel
description: Named after King Charles II, who was rarely seen without several of these spaniels at his heels, the Cavalier King Charles Spaniel was reconstructed in the 1920s from old royal portraits to restore the longer-nosed look of the original dogs. They combine the gentle temperament of a lap dog with the athletic ability of a sporting spaniel, equally comfortable on a sofa or trotting through the park. Mitral valve disease affects the majority of Cavaliers by age 10, making cardiac health screening of breeding parents an absolute minimum requirement.

conditions_detail.name: Mitral Valve Disease (MVD)
conditions_detail.description: MVD is the most common heart disease in dogs, characterised by progressive degeneration of the mitral valve leaflets causing regurgitation of blood back into the left atrium. It leads to cardiac enlargement and eventually congestive heart failure.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: The EPIC and PROTECT trials established that Pimobendan delays onset of heart failure in preclinical stages. Once failure develops, diuretics, ACE inhibitors, and Pimobendan are combined.
prevention_tips: Follow Cavalier Health cardiac breeding protocol
Breed only from clear parents over recommended age

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings
